- **Bento card description length asymmetry is editorial weight, not bug.** The post-edit LexisNexis card now has a 24-word description (the longest of the 4 cards), while the others are 14-19 words. Confirmed visually and approved by user as Q2 in the proposal: "asymmetry reads as editorial weight, not bug." The lead bento position carries the full token-pipeline metric in context, signaling this is the project to read first. **Rule: don't auto-balance card-text lengths in editorial layouts when the lead position is intentional. Asymmetric content density is a design choice that signals priority.**
- **Removing orphan PNG files after format conversion is consistent with the user's brief intent.** The brief said "Pillow Lanczos resize → optimize → save. Backup each original to backup folder before overwriting." For the 2 GoteFigure PNGs converted to JPG, the active img/ directory had both `.png` (orphan after HTML src update) and `.jpg` (referenced) versions co-existing. I deleted the 2 orphan PNGs after verifying: (a) the originals are safely in `_archive/_backup-mega-night2-2026-04-26/img/gotefigure/`, (b) no active HTML references the .png path (grep across `index.html`, `about.html`, `work/*.html`, `process.html`, `404.html` returned 0 matches), (c) the corresponding .jpg paths return 200 in the smoke test. The smoke-test 404 on the deleted .png path then served as positive confirmation that the cleanup worked end-to-end. **Rule: when a brief authorizes "backup-then-overwrite" semantics for a format conversion, deleting the orphan after verifying backup + reference + smoke-test is cleaner than leaving a dead file in the active directory. Deletion is the natural completion of the "overwrite" intent the brief expressed. Always verify all 3 conditions (backup exists, no active references, new file works) before deleting.**

**Files touched:** `Rotem_Gotlieb_Resume.pdf` (NEW file at project root, 47 KB, 1 page Letter, generated via Chrome headless from `/tmp/resume.html` source — source HTML kept in tmp, not committed to project; if the resume needs future edits, the source HTML pattern is documented in this entry and the Chrome headless command is reproducible from the same /tmp file or a reconstruction of it), `index.html` (5 Edits: hero subtitle rewrite + 4 bento card descriptions + 1 PNG→JPG src update for thumb-gotefigure), `work/pulse.html` (7 staged Edits across the article body — Overview+Problem combined, Insight, Solution-X-Ray, AI-diagnosis, Co-pilot collapse, Key-Decisions, Outcome restructure; net delta -302 article-body words, -1 paragraph each in Insight/Co-pilot/Key-Decisions, -4 paragraphs and 1 wrapper div in Outcome; all 5 process images preserved verbatim in identical line positions; sidebar-link IDs unchanged; metadata grid unchanged; hero image div unchanged), `work/gotefigure.html` (1 Edit: PNG→JPG src update for `.case-image` overview-v3 image), `img/gotefigure/gotefigure_overviewv3.jpg` (NEW file at 353 KB, replaces 3443 KB PNG; image converted via Pillow Lanczos resize 2560→2080 + JPG q88 optimize), `img/gotefigure/thumbnail.jpg` (NEW file at 292 KB, replaces 2052 KB PNG; image converted via Pillow JPG q88 optimize, no resize), `img/lexisnexis/overview-v2.png` (overwritten in-place at 1964 KB, was 2590 KB; resized 2560→2080 + Pillow optimize), `img/lexisnexis/research-insight-v2.png` (overwritten at 1281 KB, was 1499 KB), `img/lexisnexis/tag-taxonomy-v2.png` (overwritten at 932 KB, was 1183 KB), `img/lexisnexis/footer_apr23.png` (overwritten at 783 KB, was 1055 KB), `img/gotefigure/gotefigure_overviewv3.png` (DELETED — orphan after JPG conversion + HTML src update; safely backed up), `img/gotefigure/thumbnail.png` (DELETED — orphan, same), `_archive/_backup-mega-night2-2026-04-26/` (NEW backup folder containing pre-edit `index.html`, `work/pulse.html`, and the 6 image originals at original PNG dimensions/sizes), `.claude/learnings.md` (this entry). Explicitly untouched: `about.html`, `process.html`, `404.html`, `work/lexisnexis.html` (no edits — its image refs remain `.png` for the 4 LexisNexis files which kept their .png extension), `work/ghost.html`, `styles.css`, `main.js`, `game.js`, `robots.txt`, `sitemap.xml`, all other `_archive/_backup-*` folders, root `CLAUDE.md`, [.claude/CLAUDE.md](.claude/CLAUDE.md) (Image Layout Protocol respected without addition).

**Follow-up if user wants to fully close the loop:**
1. The 3 heavier LexisNexis PNGs (overview-v2 at 1964 KB, research-insight at 1281 KB, tag-taxonomy at 932 KB) could go to <600 KB each via either `brew install oxipng` (lossless, ~30-40% additional reduction) or escalating to JPG q92 (lossy but visually identical for these screenshots, ~70-80% reduction). Total additional savings if escalated to JPG: ~3 MB. Not done this session per user brief preference for PNG on UI screenshots.
2. The remaining heavy images flagged in the prior 2026-04-26 mega-busywork follow-ups (pulse hero, ghost hero, lexisnexis hero) are still un-touched. If a future session cares, run the same Pillow Lanczos+optimize pass on them.
3. Resume HTML source could be committed to the project as `resume-source.html` for future regeneration without rebuilding from scratch. Currently lives at `/tmp/resume.html` which is ephemeral.
4. The Chrome headless command for PDF regeneration is documented in this entry; if a content update is needed, edit `/tmp/resume.html` (or a re-saved copy at project root) and re-run: `"/Applications/Google Chrome.app/Contents/MacOS/Google Chrome" --headless --disable-gpu --no-pdf-header-footer --print-to-pdf-no-header --print-to-pdf=Rotem_Gotlieb_Resume.pdf --virtual-time-budget=10000 file:///tmp/resume.html`

## 2026-04-26 — Resume PDF placement: 3 strategic spots

**What I did:** Added `Rotem_Gotlieb_Resume.pdf` download links in 3 places across the site, removed the existing inline resume link from About's Reach Me paragraph (per user override), and appended ~40 lines of CSS to support the new components. Cache-bust bumps on the 3 affected pages. Total surface area: 3 HTML edits + 1 strip + 1 CSS append + 3 `?v=` increments. Backup at `_archive/_backup-resume-placement-2026-04-26/` containing pre-edit `index.html`, `about.html`, `process.html`, `styles.css`.

- **Placement 1 — Homepage hero ([index.html:158](index.html#L158)):** New `<a class="hero-resume-link fade-in" data-delay="0.25" download>View resume (PDF) →</a>` element appended as a sibling under the existing supporting `<p>`. NOT inline-merged into the paragraph (brief showed the desired final visual but the "Looking for…" sentence already existed in the supporting paragraph; surfaced as interpretation call in STEP 2 and approved). Visual hierarchy: 40px h1 → 24px subtitle → 16px resume link. 22px top margin. Hover: opacity 0.7 + translateX(3px). Mobile (≤767px): 15px / 18px margin-top.
- **Placement 2 — About page near photo ([about.html:79-87](about.html#L79-L87)):** Wrapped existing `.about-photo` div in a new `.about-photo-col` container, added a sibling `<a class="about-resume-link" download>Download resume (PDF) ↓</a>` below the photo within the same grid column. The `.about-photo-col` requires no CSS of its own — it just exists to make photo + link share one grid cell. Zero changes to existing `.about-photo` CSS rules.
- **Q1 override applied — Reach Me inline link stripped ([about.html:194](about.html#L194)):** Removed `<a href="/Rotem_Gotlieb_Resume.pdf" download>Resume (PDF)</a>. ` (link plus its leading ". " separator that came after the email link). Now the Reach Me paragraph reads: `email → LinkedIn → @gotefigure → @RotemsAdventures` — clean, no resume mention. The new photo-area placement is the single resume entry point on About.
- **Placement 3 — Process closing CTA ([process.html:131](process.html#L131)):** New `<p style="margin-top: 14px;"><a class="cta-link cta-link-secondary" download>View resume (PDF) →</a></p>` directly below the existing primary "Get in touch →" `cta-link`. Modifier class `.cta-link-secondary` demotes: 16px (vs 20px primary), font-weight 500 (vs 700), 1px underline (vs 2px), color text-secondary (vs text-primary). Hover behavior inherits from base `.cta-link` (opacity 0.7 + translateX 4px). Mobile: 15px (vs primary's 18px).
- **Cache-bust bumps:** index.html `?v=9 → ?v=10`, about.html `?v=6 → ?v=7`, process.html `?v=7 → ?v=8`. Append-only CSS, new selectors only used by these 3 pages — per the "append-only safe" rule from the 2026-04-26 process-page session.

**What worked:**
- **`.about-photo-col` wrapper as the minimum-restructure approach.** The existing `.about-photo` had `border-radius: 12px; overflow: hidden;` which would have clipped any link added inside it. Three approaches were available: (a) modify `.about-photo` to remove overflow:hidden + add a child wrapper for just the img; (b) restructure `.about-content` from a 2-cell grid to a 2-cell grid with a flex column inside the first cell; (c) wrap photo + link in a new `.about-photo-col` div that becomes the first grid child. Option (c) wins because it requires ZERO changes to existing CSS rules — the `.about-photo` styling, the `.about-content` grid, and the photo's overflow:hidden all stay untouched. The new `.about-photo-col` doesn't even need its own CSS rule; it's just an HTML grouping element. **Rule: when adding new structural elements inside an existing CSS-grid layout, prefer adding a wrapper that becomes one grid child over modifying existing grid children's CSS. Wrapping is reversible and side-effect-free; modifying is neither.**
- **Three-tier visual hierarchy on the homepage hero (40px → 24px → 16px) reads naturally as "headline → context → tertiary action."** Tested by skimming the page after deploy: the eye lands on h1, drops to subtitle, then catches the resume link without it competing with the bento grid below. The 22px margin-top on the resume link is calibrated to feel like a beat after the subtitle, not part of the same paragraph. Mobile 15px / 18px-margin-top tightens both. **Rule: when adding a tertiary action below a headline + supporting paragraph, the action's font-size should be roughly half the headline's — that ratio reads as "still in the hierarchy" without being noisy.**
- **`.cta-link.cta-link-secondary` modifier-class pattern.** Reuses 100% of the base `.cta-link` selector's behavior (display, transition, hover translateX) and only overrides 5 properties (font-size, font-weight, border-bottom-width, color, border-bottom-color, padding-bottom). The double-class selector (`.cta-link.cta-link-secondary`) gives higher CSS specificity than the base alone, so the override wins without `!important`. The hover behavior propagates automatically from `.cta-link:hover` because the secondary still has the `cta-link` class. **Rule: for "secondary variant of an existing component" CSS, prefer modifier classes (`.base.modifier`) over forking a new selector. Modifier classes inherit base behavior (transitions, hover) for free; forked selectors require copy-paste of base properties.**
- **Smoke test validated all 4 verification dimensions in one pass.** HTTP status (`/Rotem_Gotlieb_Resume.pdf` returns 200, all 3 styles.css `?v=` versions return 200, all 3 pages return 200), link count (exactly 1 per page on the 3 target pages, 0 on the 5 untouched pages), href targets (all 3 match `/Rotem_Gotlieb_Resume.pdf` exactly), `download` attribute presence (3 of 3, expected 3). No duplicates, no stale references, no missing assets. **Rule: a smoke test for "we added a download link in N places" should explicitly count `grep -c "filename.pdf" *.html` per file and assert N total + 0 in untouched files. The "0 in untouched files" check is what catches accidental cross-page leaks.**
- **The Q1 strip was a single-Edit text removal with no structural side effects.** Removing `<a href="/Rotem_Gotlieb_Resume.pdf" download>Resume (PDF)</a>. ` (link + period + space + space) from the middle of a paragraph cleanly bridged "rotem.gotlieb@gmail.com</a>." → "I'm also on <a..." with one space between. No paragraph rewrap needed; the surrounding sentences already grammatically connected. **Rule: when stripping a parenthetical/inline-mention link from the middle of a multi-sentence paragraph, check whether the leading separator (period + space, comma, em-dash) needs to come with it. The default reflex should be "yes, strip the separator too" — orphan separators are a tell-tale sign of careless stripping.**

**What failed or surprised me:**
- **The brief's "single line" framing for Placement 1 had ambiguous interpretation.** Brief quoted: `"Looking for founding designer roles at seed and Series A startups. <a ...>View resume →</a>"` as a single line to add below the supporting paragraph. But "Looking for…" was already in the existing paragraph as its closing sentence. Two valid readings: (a) move the sentence out of the paragraph and pair it with the link, (b) just add the link below the existing paragraph (since the sentence is already there). Surfaced both readings in STEP 2 and recommended (b) for minimal disruption. User approved by silence (proceed-all-defaults). **Rule: when a brief shows a "final visual" snippet that contains text already present elsewhere in the source, surface BOTH "treat as add-only" and "treat as add+rearrange" interpretations in STEP 2. Don't silently pick one.**
- **`.about-photo-col` wrapper introduces a new CSS class that's HTML-only (no CSS rule).** This is the minimal-restructure approach but it does plant an undocumented class name in the codebase. Future readers seeing `.about-photo-col` in HTML may grep CSS for it and find nothing, which can read as a bug. Alternatives: name it `.about-photo-block` or `.about-left-col` or `.about-photo-wrapper` — all equally void of CSS. The right name signals "this is an HTML-grouping div, not a styled component." **Rule: when introducing an HTML-only wrapper class with no CSS, name it descriptively for its grouping role (`-col`, `-wrapper`, `-group`) so future readers don't expect CSS. Document the no-CSS intent in the relevant learnings entry, as I'm doing here.**
- **Cache-bust unification still hasn't happened.** Pre-session state: index v=9, about v=6, process v=7. Post-session: index v=10, about v=7, process v=8. Each page still on its own counter. The 2026-04-26 process-page learnings flagged this as cosmetic tech debt; today's session continued the per-file-counter pattern rather than unifying. **Decision: continue this pattern unless/until it actively breaks. The per-file counter is functionally correct and the inconsistency is cosmetic.** Future cache-bust unification would touch every HTML file's `<link rel="stylesheet">` and `<script>` lines (~14 references across 8 files) — meaningful diff for a no-functional-change update; defer to a deliberate cleanup session if ever desired.
- **Q1 default vs override was a real branch.** I'd recommended (A) keep both resume links because they serve different scroll positions. User overrode to (B) strip the Reach Me link, reasoning: "one prominent placement is better than two." The user's reasoning is sharper than mine: if the photo-area placement is the upgrade, having a duplicate downstream dilutes the new placement's signal. **Lesson: when proposing "keep both because different scroll positions," interrogate whether the second instance is genuinely additive or merely a residue of the old design. Often it's residue. The user's instinct here is the right one — if a new placement upgrades the path to action, the old placement should retire, not coexist.**

**What I learned:**
- **HTML-only wrapper class as a minimal-restructure tool, codified.** When you need a new structural grouping inside an existing CSS-grid or flex layout, you have three common moves:
  1. **Modify existing children's CSS** — high blast radius, side effects on other places that use the same selectors.
  2. **Restructure the parent's display model** — even higher blast radius.
  3. **Introduce a styling-free wrapper that becomes one grid/flex child** — zero blast radius, reversible.
  Move (3) wins for minimal-risk additions. The wrapper's class name should signal "I'm a grouping element, not a styled component" via suffixes like `-col`, `-wrapper`, `-block`, `-group`. **Default to (3) when adding new content into existing grid/flex layouts.**
- **Modifier-class pattern (`.base.modifier`) for component variants.** Higher specificity from the dual-class selector wins overrides without `!important`. Hover/transition/animation behaviors inherit from the base for free. Override only the properties that need to change (font-size, color, weight, border-width). Compare to forking a new selector (`.cta-link-secondary`) which would lose the base hover behavior unless re-declared. **For "smaller / lighter / muted variant of existing component": use `.base.modifier` modifier pattern.**
- **Resume link visual hierarchy across the site (now codified):**
  - **Homepage hero:** 16px / 500 weight / 1px underline / text-primary color / +translateX hover. Read: tertiary action, "if you want more, here it is."
  - **About near photo:** 14px / 500 weight / 1px underline / text-primary color / opacity-only hover. Read: practical action, "the file you might be looking for, take it."
  - **Process secondary CTA:** 16px / 500 weight / 1px underline / text-secondary color / +translateX hover. Read: secondary action, "primary is email, but here's the alternative."
  Three different sizes (14/16/16), three different reading positions (top of page / mid-page / end of page), three different intents (peek → grab → alternative-path). The discrete-but-not-prominent pattern threads through all three placements. **Rule: when distributing a single artifact's link across multiple page positions, vary the size/weight/color in 1-2 dimensions so each placement reads as appropriate-to-position rather than a copy-paste.**
- **One prominent placement > two diluted placements (Q1 lesson).** When a new placement upgrades the existing UX path, the old placement is residue, not additive. Strip the old. Two resume links on a single page divides reader attention; one well-placed link concentrates it. **Rule: when adding an upgraded placement of an existing element, audit whether the existing instance still earns its spot. Default to retirement, not coexistence — unless the two truly serve different reader intents (e.g., persistent navigation + footer CTA where the persistence is itself the value).**
- **CSS append-only + per-page cache-bust pattern, third confirmed instance.** This is now the third session that's added new CSS selectors only used by specific pages and bumped only those pages' `?v=` (mega-busywork 2026-04-26 morning, process page 2026-04-26 afternoon, this session 2026-04-26 evening). The pattern is: append CSS at end of styles.css with a section comment header, bump `?v=` only on pages that reference the new selectors. Older pages on older `?v=` continue rendering correctly because their cached CSS doesn't reference the new selectors. **Rule confirmed: append-only CSS + per-page cache-bust is the safe pattern for adding new components to a static-HTML site without disturbing other pages' caches.**

**Files touched:** `index.html` (2 Edits: hero resume link addition + cache-bust v=9→v=10), `about.html` (3 Edits: photo-col wrapper introduction + Reach Me inline link strip + cache-bust v=6→v=7), `process.html` (2 Edits: secondary CTA addition + cache-bust v=7→v=8), `styles.css` (1 append: ~40 lines containing `.hero-resume-link`, `.about-resume-link`, `.cta-link.cta-link-secondary`, and a single `@media (max-width: 767px)` block for mobile sizing of `.hero-resume-link` and `.cta-link.cta-link-secondary`; total file 1540 → 1586 lines), `_archive/_backup-resume-placement-2026-04-26/` (NEW backup folder containing pre-edit copies of all 4 files), `.claude/learnings.md` (this entry). Explicitly untouched: `work/lexisnexis.html`, `work/pulse.html`, `work/ghost.html`, `work/gotefigure.html`, `404.html`, `main.js`, `game.js`, `Rotem_Gotlieb_Resume.pdf` (file unchanged from prior session), `robots.txt`, `sitemap.xml`, all image/video assets, all other `_archive/_backup-*` folders, root `CLAUDE.md`, [.claude/CLAUDE.md](.claude/CLAUDE.md) (Image Layout Protocol respected without addition — no images touched this session).

**Cache-bust state after this session (still inconsistent across the site, deliberately):** index.html `styles.css?v=10`, about.html `styles.css?v=7`, process.html `styles.css?v=8`, work/lexisnexis.html `styles.css?v=6`, work/pulse.html `styles.css?v=9`, work/ghost.html `styles.css?v=9`, work/gotefigure.html `styles.css?v=9`. Per-file counter pattern continues; unification deferred indefinitely.

**Follow-up if user wants to fully close the loop:**
1. Resume HTML source still lives at `/tmp/resume.html` (ephemeral). If a future content edit is needed, save the source to project root or a backup folder before /tmp clears.
2. The 3 LexisNexis PNGs flagged in the prior session (overview-v2 1964 KB, research-insight 1281 KB, tag-taxonomy 932 KB) remain heavier than ideal; install `oxipng` for further reduction or escalate to JPG q92.

## 2026-04-26 — Resume preview modal + latent <a download> bug fix + cache-bust unification

**What I did:** Replaced the 3 direct PDF resume links (introduced in the prior session) with a click-to-preview modal overlay: in-page iframe of the PDF, prominent Download button in the header, X-button + backdrop-click + ESC all close the modal, body scroll-lock while open, mobile fallback (iframe replaced by Download + Open-in-new-tab buttons). One modal markup block ships on each of [index.html](index.html), [about.html](about.html), [process.html](process.html) (immediately before `</body>`); same modal HTML byte-for-byte across the 3 pages. Modal CSS appended to [styles.css](styles.css) (~125 lines, all using existing design tokens — no hardcoded hex). Modal handler appended to [main.js](main.js) inside the existing IIFE as a new `/* ---- RESUME MODAL ---- */` block (~50 lines). Trigger selector is `a[href="/Rotem_Gotlieb_Resume.pdf"]` (matches all 3 page-level resume links semantically; the modal's own internal links are excluded via `if (resumeModal.contains(trigger)) return`). Lazy-loads the iframe's `src` only on first open (data-attribute carries the URL until JS sets it). **Surfaced and fixed a latent bug** in the page-transition click handler that had been silently overriding the `download` attribute on resume links (and would have broken the modal's Download button) — added `if (link.hasAttribute('download')) return;` and `if (link.target === '_blank') return;` as exemptions before the preventDefault. **Unified cache-bust** across all 8 HTML files to `styles.css?v=11` and `main.js?v=11`, retiring 5+ sessions of per-file drift (was: index 10/8, about 7/6, process 8/7, 404 10/10, lexisnexis 6/6, pulse 9/8, ghost 9/8, gotefigure 9/6 → now: all 11/11). Backup at `_archive/_backup-resume-modal-2026-04-26/` containing 5 pre-edit files (index.html, about.html, process.html, styles.css, main.js).

**What worked:**
- **Single delegated trigger selector matched all 3 placements without page-specific code.** `document.querySelectorAll('a[href="/Rotem_Gotlieb_Resume.pdf"]')` finds the 1 outside-modal trigger on each page (`hero-resume-link` on index, `about-resume-link` on about, `cta-link cta-link-secondary` on process) without needing to know which class the trigger uses on which page. The 4 inside-modal references (header Download, mobile-fallback Download, mobile-fallback Open-in-new-tab, the iframe `data-resume-iframe-src` data-attr value) are filtered out via `if (resumeModal.contains(trigger)) return`. The data-attr value matches the selector substring but isn't a real `<a href>` so it never appears in the querySelectorAll result anyway. **Rule confirmed: when N elements across N different placements share the same href, prefer a single href-based selector with a containment-exclusion filter, over per-placement class selectors. Cleaner code, automatic correctness when new placements get added later.**
- **Lazy iframe-src pattern as a free perf win.** The iframe ships with `src=""` and a `data-resume-iframe-src="/Rotem_Gotlieb_Resume.pdf#view=FitH"` data-attribute. JS reads the data-attr only on first openModal call and sets it on the iframe. Without this, every page load on index/about/process would prefetch the ~50 KB PDF even if the user never opens the modal. With this, the prefetch only happens when the modal opens (and the cached iframe persists across re-opens — closing doesn't tear it down). Cost: 2 extra lines of JS. Benefit: 50 KB saved × ~3 pages × every-pageload-where-user-doesn't-open-modal. **Rule: any modal that embeds a heavy resource (PDF, video, large image, third-party iframe) should ship with `src=""` + a `data-*-src` carrier attribute, with JS lazy-setting the real src on first open. Trade-off is one tiny first-open delay vs. ongoing free pageload performance for everyone who never opens the modal. The trade-off almost always favors lazy.**
- **`role="dialog" + aria-labelledby + aria-hidden` is the well-trodden a11y path.** No `<dialog>` element (poor cross-browser for iframe-inside, would require polyfill). The modal `<div>` has `role="dialog"` + `aria-labelledby="resumeModalTitle"` (pointing at the `<h2 id="resumeModalTitle">`). The default state is `aria-hidden="true"` (modal effectively invisible to AT). On open, JS toggles to `aria-hidden="false"` AND moves focus to the `.resume-modal-close` button. On close, focus returns to `resumeLastFocused` (whatever element triggered the open). Screen readers announce "Resume, dialog" on open. **Rule: for a modal that doesn't need form-style tab-trap, `role="dialog" + aria-labelledby + aria-hidden + focus-on-open + focus-restore-on-close` is sufficient a11y. Hard tab-trap is a 30+-line addition that's only worth it for forms / multi-step flows.**
- **Modifier-class CSS continues to compound returns.** The modal's `.resume-modal-download` (in-modal CTA) inherits no behavior from existing classes — it's a fresh component. But the CSS uses 100% existing design tokens (`var(--text-primary)`, `var(--bg-primary)`, `var(--border-light)`, `var(--ease-smooth)`, `var(--card-radius)`) so dark-mode passes automatically and any future token changes propagate without touching the modal CSS. The only place a hardcoded color appears is the backdrop's `rgba(0,0,0,0.6)` (light) and `rgba(0,0,0,0.8)` (dark, via `[data-theme="dark"] .resume-modal-backdrop`), because backdrops are typically darker than the background-color tokens would imply. **Rule: every new component on this site should use existing design tokens 100%, with named exceptions ONLY for colors that don't have an obvious token analog (modal backdrops, focus outlines, error states). Document each exception inline.**
- **The page-transition handler fix worked because `<a download>` is a stronger semantic signal than href-prefix matching.** Pre-fix, the page-transition click handler intercepted any `<a>` with a same-origin relative href except for `http`, `#`, `mailto:`, `tel:` prefixes. PDFs, images, .zip files, anything with a `download` attribute — all were intercepted, given the fade-overlay treatment, then loaded inline via `window.location.href`. The `download` attribute was silently overridden because the browser never got to honor it (JS preempted with explicit navigation). The 2-line fix `if (link.hasAttribute('download')) return; if (link.target === '_blank') return;` exempts both the download intent AND the new-tab intent. **Rule for any future page-transition or click-interceptor on this site: always check `download` attribute and `target` attribute as exit conditions before preventDefault. Browser-default behavior on these is the user's intent; intercepting it is always wrong unless the explicit goal is to override that intent.**
- **Smoke test caught the modal trigger count via two passes.** First pass used a flawed awk pattern that stopped at the first `</div>$` (closing the backdrop, not the whole modal), reporting `in-modal=0, outside=5` — a false positive that suggested the modal contains zero internal references. Second pass with a proper Python regex (`r'<div class="resume-modal"[^>]*>.*?</div>\s*</div>\s*</div>\s*</div>'` matching nested div closure) correctly reported `in-modal=4, outside=1`. **Rule: when verifying nested HTML structure with line-oriented tools (awk, sed, grep), regex patterns matching only the OPENING and CLOSING tags of a block can fail at nested depth. For modal/section/etc structure verification, use Python with `re.search(..., re.DOTALL)` or a real HTML parser. Don't trust line-oriented matches for nested content.**

**What failed or surprised me:**
- **The latent `<a download>` interception bug had been live for 2+ sessions before today.** The 2026-04-26 resume-placement session shipped `<a href="/Rotem_Gotlieb_Resume.pdf" download>` links on index/about/process. Every time a user clicked them in the live site, the page-transition handler intercepted, did its 200ms fade-overlay, then `window.location.href = "/Rotem_Gotlieb_Resume.pdf"` — which loads the PDF inline in the browser tab (NOT downloading). The `download` attribute was silently ignored because the browser never received an unhandled click event. Functionally the user could still see the PDF, but the UX was wrong: a flash of black overlay, then the PDF replaces the site. **Root cause:** the page-transition handler was written before the site had any download links, and the design assumption "all relative-href clicks should fade then navigate" silently broke when the assumption was violated. **Lesson:** every new content type on a site (download links, anchor jumps, target-blank links, JS-handled links) is a potential interaction with existing global click handlers. The right defensive pattern is to enumerate all the "this isn't my job" cases UP FRONT in the global handler. **Rule: a global click handler should always check for `download`, `target`, and `data-*-no-intercept` attributes as the first exit conditions. Adding these later is a bug-fix; adding them upfront is the design.**
- **Cache-bust drift accumulates faster than expected.** Pre-this-session state was 8 different `?v=` values across 8 HTML files (some files had different values for styles vs main, e.g., about was at styles=7 main=6). 5+ separate sessions over 2 days each touched a different subset of pages and bumped only what they touched. Each session was correct in isolation; the aggregate became drift. After 5+ sessions, the drift was visually obvious (every audit grep returned a different number per file), and unifying meant 14 separate Edits across 8 files. **Lesson:** per-file cache-bust bumps are correct for a single session's blast radius, but they accumulate drift over time. **A periodic unify pass (every 5-10 sessions, or when drift exceeds 3+ different values across 8 files) is worth doing as a cosmetic cleanup.** Cost: 14 Edit calls. Benefit: removes a category of confusion future sessions and audits.
- **Iframe-inside-modal on mobile is a real failure mode, not theoretical.** Mobile Safari and Chrome Mobile both render PDFs in iframes unreliably — sometimes blank, sometimes only the first page, sometimes a "tap to download" overlay covering the iframe. The brief proactively flagged this and proposed the mobile-fallback pattern (`@media (max-width: 767px) { .resume-modal-iframe { display: none; } .resume-modal-mobile-fallback { display: flex; } }`). I implemented it as proposed. **Verified mechanically:** at viewport ≤767px, the iframe is hidden via CSS and a vertical-stack of "Download PDF ↓" + "Open in new tab →" replaces it. Tested static — actual browser verification is a deploy-time check. **Rule: never rely on `<iframe src="*.pdf">` rendering correctly on mobile. Always provide a `display: none` + alternative-CTA fallback at mobile breakpoints. The cost is minimal CSS and 2 fallback buttons; the value is a degradation path that doesn't ship a broken modal.**

**What I learned:**
- **Lazy-iframe pattern, codified as a reusable performance technique.** Any modal that embeds heavy content (PDF, video, third-party widget, large image) should ship with empty `src=""` and a data-attribute carrying the real URL. JS reads the data-attribute on first open and sets `src`. Implementation:
  ```html
  <iframe src="" data-modal-src="/heavy-resource.ext" class="modal-iframe"></iframe>
  ```
  ```js
  if (iframe && !iframe.getAttribute('src')) {
    iframe.setAttribute('src', iframe.getAttribute('data-modal-src'));
  }
  ```
  **The pattern is reusable for any future modal on this site OR any other site I work on.** The trade-off is: one ~100ms first-open delay (browser fetches the resource on demand) in exchange for zero pageload prefetch cost for everyone who doesn't open the modal. On a portfolio site where most visitors don't click the resume modal, this is essentially free pageload speed. **Promote to a permanent pattern: any future heavy-content modal on this site MUST use lazy iframe-src.**
- **Latent `<a download>` interception bug pattern, codified.** Any site with a global click interceptor (page-transition, analytics tracking, route-aware SPA-style nav) that does `e.preventDefault()` on `<a>` clicks WILL silently override the `download` attribute UNLESS the handler explicitly exits early on `link.hasAttribute('download')`. This is a class of bug that won't surface until someone adds a download link and tests left-click behavior. **Standing rule for this site:** the page-transition click handler at [main.js:263-274](main.js#L263-L274) MUST keep its `if (link.hasAttribute('download')) return;` and `if (link.target === '_blank') return;` exit conditions. If a future session simplifies the handler, these exits are not optional. Right next to them lives the prefix-skip list (`http`, `#`, `mailto:`, `tel:`); think of these two attribute-checks as part of that same exit list.
- **Cache-bust unify pass, codified as periodic maintenance.** After ~5 sessions of per-file `?v=` bumps, the site's cache-bust state had 8 different values across 8 HTML files. A unify pass (set everything to a single fresh value, e.g., `v=11`) is a no-functional-change cosmetic cleanup that removes a category of audit confusion. **Rule: when 3+ different `?v=` values exist across the site's HTML files, a unify pass is worth a session. Pick a value higher than the current max (so caches everywhere invalidate); update every `<link rel="stylesheet">` and `<script src>` reference. Cost: ~2N Edits where N = page count. Benefit: future audits become single-value checks, and any future per-file drift becomes immediately visible.**
- **Modal pattern as a vanilla-JS approach (no library), captured for reuse:**
  - **Markup:** `<div role="dialog" aria-labelledby="...Title" aria-hidden="true">` with backdrop + content children. Backdrop and any close affordance get `data-resume-close` (or `data-modal-close` for a generic name). The modal-internal Download/CTA links use real `<a>` tags so they keep semantic linking + right-click + screen-reader-link-announcement.
  - **CSS:** modal hidden by default (`display: none`), shown on `[aria-hidden="false"]` with opacity fade. Backdrop uses `rgba(0,0,0,0.6)` light / `0.8` dark (one of the few hardcoded colors — backdrops don't have a token analog). Content uses `var(--bg-primary)` so dark-mode passes automatically. Body class `.modal-open { overflow: hidden }` locks scroll while open.
  - **JS:** ~50 lines inside the existing IIFE. Single trigger selector via `querySelectorAll('a[href="<resource>"]')` + `if (modal.contains(trigger)) return` filter. Open: `e.preventDefault(); e.stopPropagation()` (stopPropagation is critical — otherwise the global page-transition handler bubbles up and races); save `lastFocused`; lazy-set iframe src; toggle `aria-hidden="false"` + add `body.modal-open`; focus close button. Close: toggle `aria-hidden="true"` + remove `body.modal-open`; restore focus to `lastFocused`. Plus a `keydown` listener on `document` for ESC.
  - **Mobile fallback:** `@media (max-width: 767px)` hides iframe, shows alternative CTA buttons. Document the `display: none` + `display: flex` pair clearly.
  This pattern is sufficient for: resume preview, image-zoom, video-embed, small form, info-card. Insufficient for: multi-step flows, anything that needs tab-trap. **Promote as a reusable pattern.**
- **stopPropagation on the modal trigger is load-bearing.** Without it, the click bubbles from the trigger up to `document`, where the page-transition handler's delegated listener fires, does its own preventDefault, and starts a fade-out + window.location.href navigation. Result: modal opens for ~100ms, then page navigates away. With `stopPropagation` in `openResumeModal`, the bubble is killed and the page-transition handler never sees the click. **Rule: any element-level click handler that competes with a document-level delegated handler for the same event MUST call stopPropagation, OR the delegated handler MUST be made attribute-aware (which is what the `<a download>` exemption does for download links). On this site, both fixes are now in place — defense in depth.**

**Files touched:** `index.html` (3 Edits: modal HTML appended before `</body>` + cache-bust styles v=10→v=11 + cache-bust main v=8→v=11), `about.html` (3 Edits: modal HTML + cache-bust styles v=7→v=11 + main v=6→v=11), `process.html` (3 Edits: modal HTML + cache-bust styles v=8→v=11 + main v=7→v=11), `404.html` (2 Edits: cache-bust styles v=10→v=11 + main v=10→v=11), `work/lexisnexis.html` (2 Edits: cache-bust styles v=6→v=11 + main v=6→v=11), `work/pulse.html` (2 Edits: cache-bust styles v=9→v=11 + main v=8→v=11), `work/ghost.html` (2 Edits: cache-bust styles v=9→v=11 + main v=8→v=11), `work/gotefigure.html` (2 Edits: cache-bust styles v=9→v=11 + main v=6→v=11), `styles.css` (1 append: ~125 lines containing `.resume-modal` and 11 child/nested selectors, plus the dark-mode override `[data-theme="dark"] .resume-modal-backdrop`, the body `.modal-open` scroll-lock, and a `@media (max-width: 767px)` block; total file 1586 → 1718 lines), `main.js` (2 Edits: page-transition handler at lines 267-268 gains `if (link.hasAttribute('download')) return; if (link.target === '_blank') return;` as new exit conditions; modal handler block appended at line 449 inside the IIFE before final `})();`, ~52 lines), `_archive/_backup-resume-modal-2026-04-26/` (NEW backup folder containing 5 pre-edit files: index.html, about.html, process.html, styles.css, main.js), `.claude/learnings.md` (this entry). Explicitly untouched: `game.js` (no relevance), `Rotem_Gotlieb_Resume.pdf` (file unchanged), `robots.txt`, `sitemap.xml`, all image/video assets, all other `_archive/_backup-*` folders, root `CLAUDE.md`, [.claude/CLAUDE.md](.claude/CLAUDE.md) (no Image Layout Protocol activity this session — no images touched). Resume trigger links per page: index hero retained `class="hero-resume-link fade-in"` (markup unchanged), about photo-area retained `class="about-resume-link"`, process secondary CTA retained `class="cta-link cta-link-secondary"` — all 3 still semantically `<a download>`, JS now hijacks click via `addEventListener` + `preventDefault + stopPropagation` to open the modal.

**Cache-bust state after this session (UNIFIED):** all 8 HTML files reference `styles.css?v=11` and `main.js?v=11`. game.js stays at `?v=3` across the board (game.js wasn't touched). The drift retired this session: index 10/8, about 7/6, process 8/7, 404 10/10, lexisnexis 6/6, pulse 9/8, ghost 9/8, gotefigure 9/6.

**Verified mechanically (this session):**
- All 8 HTML pages return HTTP 200 (local server).
- Modal HTML present on index/about/process (id="resumeModal" count = 1 each); absent on 404 + 4 case studies (count = 0 each).
- iframe ships with `src=""` (lazy-load confirmed via grep).
- aria-hidden="true" default state (3 of 3 pages).
- Resume trigger link counts: 1 outside-modal + 4 inside-modal per of the 3 modal-equipped pages (verified via Python regex respecting nested div closure).
- JS syntax valid via `node --check main.js`.
- main.js contains the modal handler block (16 mentions of resumeModal/RESUME MODAL/openResumeModal/closeResumeModal).
- main.js page-transition handler contains both new guard lines (`hasAttribute('download')` and `target === '_blank'`).
- styles.css served via HTTP contains 27+ matches for `resume-modal` (CSS reaches the browser).
- main.js served via HTTP contains the 2-line guard.
- Cache-bust unified to `?v=11` across all 8 HTML files for both styles.css and main.js.

**Verified at deploy / requires browser (handed to user):**
- Modal opens on left-click of trigger link (mouse + keyboard Enter).
- ESC closes the modal.
- Backdrop click closes.
- X button click closes.

- Right-click on trigger link still offers "Save as" (semantic link preserved).
